description_long: A group of six cardboard parcels of varying sizes is arranged in two clusters, with three boxes stacked and three placed side by side. The boxes are made of brown corrugated cardboard, featuring visible fold lines, flaps, and printed recycling symbols on some surfaces. The parcels have a slightly worn appearance, with subtle creases and minor surface imperfections typical of handled shipping boxes. The boxes are closed, with some flaps overlapping and edges neatly aligned, suggesting they are ready for transport or storage. The overall arrangement is compact, with the stacked group forming a small pile and the other group laid out in a row. These parcels are commonly seen in urban environments such as sidewalks, loading docks, or near storefronts awaiting pickup or delivery.
description: A set of six brown cardboard parcels, some stacked and some aligned in a row, with visible recycling symbols and realistic surface details.
description_view_0: Four cardboard boxes are aligned in a row on the left, while three are stacked in a compact pile on the right, showing the side and top surfaces.
description_view_1: The view from above highlights the tops of the boxes, with the stacked group in the foreground and the row of boxes behind.
description_view_2: The arrangement is reversed, with the stacked boxes on the left and the row of boxes on the right, showing more of the side surfaces.
description_view_3: A direct end-on view, showing the narrowest faces of the boxes, with the stacked group in the center and the row extending behind.
